---
title: Marginal Cost 8760 Heatmaps
reference-location: margin
fig-cap-location: bottom
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook loads the four marginal cost 8760 traces that CAIRO uses for each NY utility — energy, generation capacity, bulk transmission, and distribution + sub-transmission — and combines them into a single DataFrame for inspection. These traces are normally only assembled inside `run_scenario.py` in [rate-design-platform](https://github.com/switchbox-data/rate-design-platform); this notebook makes them directly explorable.

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

import json

import polars as pl
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    coord_cartesian,
    facet_grid,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_col,
    geom_step,
    geom_text,
    geom_tile,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    scale_fill_gradient,
    scale_fill_manual,
    scale_x_continuous,
    scale_y_continuous,
    theme,
)

from lib.plotnine import SB_COLORS, theme_switchbox
from lib.rdp import fetch_rdp_file
```

We define the seven NY utilities and the S3 path pattern for each marginal cost type. These paths match those in the scenario YAMLs at `rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/scenarios/scenarios_{utility}.yaml`.

```{python}
#| label: parameters

UTILITIES = ["cenhud", "coned", "nimo", "nyseg", "or", "psegli", "rge"]
YEAR = 2025

TARIFF_DIR = "rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/tariffs/electric"
RDP_REF = "8bf03e6"

SEASON_ENUM = pl.Enum(["Summer", "Winter"])
CAP_ENUM = pl.Enum(["Low-cost Day", "High-cost Day"])

MC_SOURCES: dict[str, dict] = {
    "mc_energy": {
        "path_template": "s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/ny/supply/energy/utility={utility}/year={year}/data.parquet",
        "source_col": "energy_cost_enduse",
        "source_unit": "$/MWh",
    },
    "mc_gen_cap": {
        "path_template": "s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/ny/supply/capacity/utility={utility}/year={year}/data.parquet",
        "source_col": "capacity_cost_enduse",
        "source_unit": "$/MWh",
    },
    "mc_bulk_tx": {
        "path_template": "s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/ny/bulk_tx/utility={utility}/year={year}/data.parquet",
        "source_col": "bulk_tx_cost_enduse",
        "source_unit": "$/kWh",
    },
    "mc_dist_sub_tx": {
        "path_template": "s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/ny/dist_and_sub_tx/utility={utility}/year={year}/data.parquet",
        "source_col": "mc_total_per_kwh",
        "source_unit": "$/kWh",
    },
}
```

```{python}
#| label: load-tou-rates-func


def _load_urdb(utility_code: str, suffix: str) -> dict:
    """Fetch and parse a single URDB tariff JSON from rate-design-platform."""
    raw = fetch_rdp_file(f"{TARIFF_DIR}/{utility_code}_{suffix}.json", ref=RDP_REF)
    return json.loads(raw)["items"][0]


def _hourly_profile(tariff: dict, schedule: list[list[int]]) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Build a season × hour DataFrame of rates ($/kWh) from a URDB tariff.

    Uses *schedule* (the ``energyweekdayschedule`` from the delivery tariff)
    to determine on-/off-peak hours — the schedule is the same across all
    calibration variants of a given TOU tariff.
    """
    rates = [tier[0]["rate"] for tier in tariff["energyratestructure"]]
    rows: list[dict] = []
    for season_name, month_idx, off_idx, on_idx in [
        ("Summer", 6, 2, 3),
        ("Winter", 0, 0, 1),
    ]:
        row = schedule[month_idx]
        off_peak_period = row[0]
        for h in range(24):
            idx = off_idx if row[h] == off_peak_period else on_idx
            rows.append({"season": season_name, "hour": h + 1, "tou_rate": rates[idx]})
    return pl.DataFrame(rows).with_columns(pl.col("season").cast(SEASON_ENUM))


def load_tou_rates(
    utility_code: str,
) -> tuple[pl.DataFrame, pl.DataFrame, pl.DataFrame]:
    """Load TOU rate profiles for a utility.

    Returns ``(bundled_cal, supply_cal, supply_precal)`` where each DataFrame
    has columns: season (SEASON_ENUM), hour (1-24), tou_rate ($/kWh).

    - *bundled_cal*: calibrated delivery+supply — the full customer rate.
    - *supply_cal*: calibrated supply-only (= bundled_cal − delivery_cal).
    - *supply_precal*: pre-calibration supply — the cost-reflective rate
      before CAIRO inflates it to recover the residual.
    """
    d = _load_urdb(utility_code, "hp_seasonalTOU_calibrated")
    b = _load_urdb(utility_code, "hp_seasonalTOU_supply_calibrated")
    pre = _load_urdb(utility_code, "hp_seasonalTOU_supply")

    schedule = d["energyweekdayschedule"]

    bundled_cal = _hourly_profile(b, schedule)
    delivery_cal = _hourly_profile(d, schedule)
    supply_cal = bundled_cal.with_columns(
        (pl.col("tou_rate") - delivery_cal["tou_rate"]).alias("tou_rate")
    )
    supply_precal = _hourly_profile(pre, schedule)

    return bundled_cal, supply_cal, supply_precal
```

## Load MCs from S3

We load each marginal cost parquet for each utility, validate that it has exactly 8760 rows with no nulls, rename the cost column to a standard name, and normalize units to $/kWh.

```{python}
#| label: load-mcs


def load_mc(
    utility: str,
    mc_name: str,
    source: dict,
    year: int,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Load one MC parquet for one utility, validate, and normalize to $/kWh."""
    path = source["path_template"].format(utility=utility, year=year)
    df = pl.read_parquet(path, storage_options={"aws_region": "us-west-2"})

    assert df.height == 8760, (
        f"{mc_name} for {utility}: expected 8760 rows, got {df.height}"
    )
    assert df[source["source_col"]].null_count() == 0, (
        f"{mc_name} for {utility}: has null values in {source['source_col']}"
    )

    result = df.select(
        pl.col("timestamp"),
        pl.col(source["source_col"]).alias(mc_name),
    )

    if source["source_unit"] == "$/MWh":
        result = result.with_columns(pl.col(mc_name) / 1000)

    return result
```

```{python}
#| label: load-all

frames: list[pl.DataFrame] = []

for utility in UTILITIES:
    mc_dfs = [
        load_mc(utility, mc_name, source, YEAR)
        for mc_name, source in MC_SOURCES.items()
    ]

    combined = mc_dfs[0]
    for mc_df in mc_dfs[1:]:
        combined = combined.join(mc_df, on="timestamp", how="inner")

    assert combined.height == 8760, (
        f"{utility}: joined MC has {combined.height} rows, expected 8760"
    )
    assert combined.null_count().row(0) == (0, 0, 0, 0, 0), (
        f"{utility}: joined MC has nulls"
    )

    frames.append(combined.with_columns(pl.lit(utility).alias("utility")))
    print(f"  {utility}: OK ({combined.height} rows)")

mc_all = pl.concat(frames)
print(f"\nCombined: {mc_all.height} rows, {mc_all.width} columns")
```

## Inspect the data

```{python}
#| label: show-schema

mc_all.head(10)
```

Annual totals per utility and MC type (in $/kW-yr — the sum of hourly $/kWh across 8760 hours):

```{python}
#| label: summary-table

mc_all.group_by("utility").agg(
    pl.col("mc_energy").sum().alias("energy"),
    pl.col("mc_gen_cap").sum().alias("gen_cap"),
    pl.col("mc_bulk_tx").sum().alias("bulk_tx"),
    pl.col("mc_dist_sub_tx").sum().alias("dist_sub_tx"),
    (
        pl.col("mc_energy")
        + pl.col("mc_gen_cap")
        + pl.col("mc_bulk_tx")
        + pl.col("mc_dist_sub_tx")
    )
    .sum()
    .alias("total"),
).sort("utility")
```

## ConEd marginal cost heatmaps

We extract day-of-year and hour-of-day from the timestamp, filter to ConEd, and melt into long format for faceting.

```{python}
#| label: prepare-heatmap-data

coned = (
    mc_all.filter(pl.col("utility") == "coned")
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.ordinal_day().alias("day_of_year"),
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.hour().alias("hour"),
    )
    .select("day_of_year", "hour", "mc_energy", "mc_gen_cap", "mc_bulk_tx", "mc_dist_sub_tx")
    .unpivot(
        index=["day_of_year", "hour"],
        variable_name="mc_type",
        value_name="value",
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("mc_type").replace_strict(
            {
                "mc_energy": "Energy",
                "mc_gen_cap": "Gen Capacity",
                "mc_bulk_tx": "Bulk Tx",
                "mc_dist_sub_tx": "Dist+Sub-Tx",
            }
        )
    )
)

coned.head()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-coned-mc-heatmaps
#| fig-cap: "Hourly marginal costs for ConEd across 2025, by cost component. Each tile is one hour; darker = higher $/kWh."

(
    ggplot(coned, aes(x="day_of_year", y="hour", fill="value"))
    + geom_tile()
    + facet_wrap("mc_type", ncol=2)
    + scale_fill_gradient(low="#FFFFFF", high=SB_COLORS["midnight"])
    + scale_x_continuous(
        breaks=[1, 91, 182, 274, 365],
        labels=["Jan", "Apr", "Jul", "Oct", "Dec"],
    )
    + scale_y_continuous(breaks=[0, 6, 12, 18, 23])
    + coord_cartesian(expand=False)
    + labs(
        title="ConEd Marginal Costs by Hour and Day ($/kWh, 2025)",
        x="",
        y="Hour (EST)",
        fill="$/kWh",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 8))
)
```

## T+D marginal cost heatmaps by utility

**Bulk transmission** and **Distribution + sub-transmission** (NYISO) marginal costs are the delivery-side $/kWh signals used in the CAIRO build. Each is stored per utility, per year. Below, the two are plotted side by side. **Each panel has its own color scale** (Bulk Tx and Dist+Sub-Tx are often on different $/kWh levels), so the seasonal and diurnal patterns in each are visible. Darker = higher $/kWh. Day-of-year and hour align to the 8760 hourly rows in the marginal cost parquet.[^td-heatmap-axes]

[^td-heatmap-axes]: The underlying parquet columns are `mc_bulk_tx` and `mc_dist_sub_tx` from `s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/ny/bulk_tx/…` and `…/ny/dist_and_sub_tx/…`.

Plotnine `facet_wrap` can only *free* the x/y axes, not the fill (color) scale, so the heat tiles here use `matplotlib` with one scale per T/D component.

```{python}
#| label: td-heatmap-matplotlib
#| code-fold: true

import matplotlib as mpl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.figure import Figure

_TD_UTILITY_TITLES = {
    "cenhud": "Central Hudson",
    "coned": "ConEd",
    "nimo": "National Grid",
    "nyseg": "NYSEG",
    "or": "O&R",
    "psegli": "PSEG-LI",
    "rge": "RG&E",
}

_TD_CMAP = mpl.colors.LinearSegmentedColormap.from_list(
    "sb_midnight", ("#FFFFFF", SB_COLORS["midnight"])
)


def plot_td_heatmap(utility: str) -> Figure:
    """2-panel hour×dayofyear heatmap: Bulk Tx, Dist+Sub-Tx, independent $/kWh color scales."""
    udf = mc_all.filter(pl.col("utility") == utility).sort("timestamp")
    assert udf.height == 8760, f"{utility}: expected 8760 hours, got {udf.height}"
    n_days = udf.height // 24
    bulk = udf["mc_bulk_tx"].to_numpy().reshape(n_days, 24).T
    dist = udf["mc_dist_sub_tx"].to_numpy().reshape(n_days, 24).T
    # bulk/shape: (24, n_days) — y = hour, x = day of year
    x_left = 1.0
    x_right = float(1 + n_days - 1)
    y_bottom = 0.0
    y_top = 24.0

    name = _TD_UTILITY_TITLES.get(utility, utility)
    title = f"{name}: T+D marginal costs ($/kWh, {YEAR})"
    fig, (ax0, ax1) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10.5, 4.0), sharey=True)
    fig.suptitle(title, fontsize=12, y=1.02)
    last_day = 1 + n_days - 1
    x_label_positions = [1, 91, 182, 274, last_day]
    x_label_strings = ["Jan", "Apr", "Jul", "Oct", "Dec"]
    for ax, data, ptitle in [
        (ax0, bulk, "Bulk transmission"),
        (ax1, dist, "Distribution + sub-transmission"),
    ]:
        im = ax.imshow(
            data,
            aspect="auto",
            origin="lower",
            cmap=_TD_CMAP,
            extent=(x_left, x_right, y_bottom, y_top),
            interpolation="nearest",
        )
        cbar = fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="$/kWh", shrink=0.9)
        cbar.ax.minorticks_off()
        ax.set_xlim(x_left, x_right)
        ax.set_ylim(y_bottom, y_top)
        ax.set_title(ptitle, fontsize=11, pad=8.0)
        ax.set_yticks([0, 6, 12, 18, 23])
        ax.set_xticks(x_label_positions, labels=x_label_strings)
    ax0.set_ylabel("Hour (clock)")
    for ax in (ax0, ax1):
        ax.set_xlabel("Day of year (calendar)")
    fig.tight_layout()
    return fig
```

### Central Hudson

```{python}
#| label: fig-td-heatmap-cenhud
#| fig-cap: "T+D marginal $/kWh by hour and day: bulk transmission and distribution+sub-transmission, Central Hudson."

plot_td_heatmap("cenhud")
```

### ConEd

```{python}
#| label: fig-td-heatmap-coned
#| fig-cap: "T+D marginal $/kWh by hour and day: bulk transmission and distribution+sub-transmission, ConEd."

plot_td_heatmap("coned")
```

### National Grid

```{python}
#| label: fig-td-heatmap-nimo
#| fig-cap: "T+D marginal $/kWh by hour and day: bulk transmission and distribution+sub-transmission, National Grid (Northeast)."

plot_td_heatmap("nimo")
```

### NYSEG

```{python}
#| label: fig-td-heatmap-nyseg
#| fig-cap: "T+D marginal $/kWh by hour and day: bulk transmission and distribution+sub-transmission, NYSEG."

plot_td_heatmap("nyseg")
```

### O&R

```{python}
#| label: fig-td-heatmap-or
#| fig-cap: "T+D marginal $/kWh by hour and day: bulk transmission and distribution+sub-transmission, Orange and Rockland."

plot_td_heatmap("or")
```

### PSEG-LI

```{python}
#| label: fig-td-heatmap-psegli
#| fig-cap: "T+D marginal $/kWh by hour and day: bulk transmission and distribution+sub-transmission, PSEG Long Island."

plot_td_heatmap("psegli")
```

### RG&E

```{python}
#| label: fig-td-heatmap-rge
#| fig-cap: "T+D marginal $/kWh by hour and day: bulk transmission and distribution+sub-transmission, Rochester Gas and Electric."

plot_td_heatmap("rge")
```

## Representative day stacked bar charts

The heatmaps above show the full 8760 but can be hard to read at a glance. Here we select four **representative days** for a utility — a low-capacity and high-capacity day in each of summer and winter — and plot them as stacked bar charts showing all four MC components by hour. This makes it easy to see the hourly shape of costs and how capacity costs dominate on peak days.

### Selection algorithm

For a given utility's 8760:

1. Define **summer** as June--August and **winter** as December--February.
2. Compute each day's total capacity cost = sum of `mc_gen_cap + mc_bulk_tx` across 24 hours.
3. Within each season, split days at the **median** daily capacity cost into a low-capacity group (below median) and a high-capacity group (at or above median).
4. For each group, compute the **mean hourly profile** — the average value of each MC at each hour of day across all days in the group. This smooths out day-to-day noise while preserving the characteristic shape.

```{python}
#| label: representative-day-algorithm

SUMMER_MONTHS = [6, 7, 8]
WINTER_MONTHS = [12, 1, 2]

MC_COLS = ["mc_energy", "mc_gen_cap", "mc_bulk_tx", "mc_dist_sub_tx"]

MC_DISPLAY_LABELS = {
    "mc_energy": "Energy",
    "mc_gen_cap": "Gen Capacity",
    "mc_bulk_tx": "Bulk Tx",
    "mc_dist_sub_tx": "Dist+Sub-Tx",
}

# plotnine stacks first enum level on top, so reverse for bottom-to-top visual order
MC_ORDER = list(reversed(MC_DISPLAY_LABELS.values()))
MC_ENUM = pl.Enum(MC_ORDER)

MC_DISPLAY_COLORS = {
    "Energy": SB_COLORS["carrot"],
    "Gen Capacity": SB_COLORS["saffron"],
    "Bulk Tx": SB_COLORS["sky"],
    "Dist+Sub-Tx": SB_COLORS["midnight"],
}


def select_representative_days(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Build mean hourly profiles for 4 representative day types.

    Returns a long-format DataFrame with columns:
    season, cap_level, hour (1-24), mc_type, value ($/kWh).
    """
    enriched = df.with_columns(
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.month().alias("month"),
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.date().alias("date"),
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.hour().alias("hour"),
    )

    daily_cap = enriched.group_by("date", "month").agg(
        (pl.col("mc_gen_cap") + pl.col("mc_bulk_tx")).sum().alias("daily_cap_cost")
    )

    results: list[pl.DataFrame] = []

    for season_name, months in [("Summer", SUMMER_MONTHS), ("Winter", WINTER_MONTHS)]:
        season_days = daily_cap.filter(pl.col("month").is_in(months))
        median_cap = season_days["daily_cap_cost"].median()

        low_dates = season_days.filter(pl.col("daily_cap_cost") <= median_cap)["date"]
        high_dates = season_days.filter(pl.col("daily_cap_cost") > median_cap)["date"]

        for level_name, dates in [("Low-cost Day", low_dates), ("High-cost Day", high_dates)]:
            group = enriched.filter(pl.col("date").is_in(dates))

            hourly_mean = (
                group.group_by("hour")
                .agg([pl.col(c).mean() for c in MC_COLS])
                .sort("hour")
                .with_columns(pl.col("hour") + 1)  # display as 1-24
            )

            long = (
                hourly_mean.unpivot(
                    index=["hour"],
                    variable_name="mc_type",
                    value_name="value",
                )
                .with_columns(
                    pl.col("mc_type").replace_strict(MC_DISPLAY_LABELS).cast(MC_ENUM),
                    pl.lit(season_name).cast(SEASON_ENUM).alias("season"),
                    pl.lit(level_name).cast(CAP_ENUM).alias("cap_level"),
                )
            )
            results.append(long)

    return pl.concat(results)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-representative-days-func

UTILITY_DISPLAY_NAMES = {
    "cenhud": "Central Hudson",
    "coned": "ConEd",
    "nimo": "National Grid",
    "nyseg": "NYSEG",
    "or": "O&R",
    "psegli": "PSEG-LI",
    "rge": "RG&E",
}


def _expand_tou_to_facets(tou: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Replicate a season × hour TOU DataFrame across both cap_level facets."""
    return pl.concat([
        tou.with_columns(pl.lit(level).cast(CAP_ENUM).alias("cap_level"))
        for level in CAP_ENUM.categories
    ])


def plot_representative_days(
    utility: str,
    tou_rates: pl.DataFrame | None = None,
    tou_rates_supply: pl.DataFrame | None = None,
    tou_rates_precal: pl.DataFrame | None = None,
    season: str = "both",
) -> None:
    """Plot faceted stacked bar chart of representative day MCs for one utility.

    *season*: ``"both"`` (2×2 grid), ``"Summer"``, or ``"Winter"`` (1×2 strip).

    Optional TOU overlays (each a season × hour DataFrame with a ``tou_rate`` column):

    - *tou_rates*: calibrated bundled (delivery+supply) — solid black line.
    - *tou_rates_supply*: calibrated supply-only — dashed black line.
    - *tou_rates_precal*: pre-calibration supply (cost-reflective) — dotted black line.
    """
    name = UTILITY_DISPLAY_NAMES.get(utility, utility)
    rep = select_representative_days(mc_all.filter(pl.col("utility") == utility))
    rep = rep.with_columns((pl.col("hour") + 0.5).alias("hour"))

    if season != "both":
        rep = rep.filter(pl.col("season") == season)

    has_tou = any(t is not None for t in [tou_rates, tou_rates_supply, tou_rates_precal])
    title_suffix = " vs. TOU Rates" if has_tou else ""
    season_label = f" — {season}" if season != "both" else ""
    fig_height = 7 if season == "both" else 4
    p = (
        ggplot(rep, aes(x="hour", y="value", fill="mc_type"))
        + geom_col(width=1.0)
        + facet_grid("season", "cap_level")
        + scale_fill_manual(values=MC_DISPLAY_COLORS)
        + scale_x_continuous(breaks=[1, 6, 12, 18, 24])
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.05, 0))
        + labs(
            title=f"{name}: {season_label} Time-of-use Prices vs. Marginal Costs ($/kWh, {YEAR})",
            x="Hour of Day",
            y="$/kWh",
            fill="",
        )
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(10.5, fig_height))
    )

    tou_lines: list[tuple[pl.DataFrame | None, str]] = [
        (tou_rates, "solid"),
        (tou_rates_supply, "dashed"),
        (tou_rates_precal, "dotted"),
    ]
    for tou, lt in tou_lines:
        if tou is not None:
            tou_data = tou if season == "both" else tou.filter(pl.col("season") == season)
            p = p + geom_step(
                _expand_tou_to_facets(tou_data),
                aes(x="hour", y="tou_rate"),
                inherit_aes=False,
                direction="hv",
                color="black",
                size=0.7,
                linetype=lt,
            )

    if tou_rates is not None:
        tou_lbl = tou_rates if season == "both" else tou_rates.filter(pl.col("season") == season)
        label_rows: list[dict] = []
        for s in tou_lbl["season"].unique().to_list():
            s_data = tou_lbl.filter(pl.col("season") == s).sort("hour")
            rates_sorted = s_data["tou_rate"].unique().sort().to_list()
            off_rate, on_rate = rates_sorted[0], rates_sorted[-1]
            off_hours = s_data.filter(pl.col("tou_rate") == off_rate)["hour"].to_list()
            on_hours = s_data.filter(pl.col("tou_rate") == on_rate)["hour"].to_list()
            label_rows.append({"season": s, "hour": float(off_hours[len(off_hours) // 4]), "y": off_rate, "label": f"Off-peak: ${off_rate:.3f}"})
            label_rows.append({"season": s, "hour": float(on_hours[len(on_hours) // 2]), "y": on_rate, "label": f"On-peak: ${on_rate:.3f}"})
        labels_df = pl.DataFrame(label_rows).with_columns(
            pl.col("season").cast(SEASON_ENUM),
            pl.lit("Low-cost Day").cast(CAP_ENUM).alias("cap_level"),
        )
        p = p + geom_text(
            labels_df, aes(x="hour", y="y", label="label"),
            inherit_aes=False, va="bottom", nudge_y=0.002, size=9, color="black",
        )

    p.show()
```

### ConEd

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-coned
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("coned")
plot_representative_days("coned", tou_rates=_bundled, tou_rates_supply=_supply, tou_rates_precal=_precal)
```

### Central Hudson

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-cenhud-summer
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("cenhud")
plot_representative_days("cenhud", tou_rates=_bundled, season="Summer")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-cenhud-winter
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("cenhud")
plot_representative_days("cenhud", tou_rates=_bundled, season="Winter")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-cenhud
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("cenhud")
plot_representative_days("cenhud", tou_rates=_bundled, season="both")
```

### National Grid

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-nimo
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("nimo")
plot_representative_days("nimo", tou_rates=_bundled, tou_rates_supply=_supply, tou_rates_precal=_precal)
```

### NYSEG

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-nyseg
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("nyseg")
plot_representative_days("nyseg", tou_rates=_bundled, tou_rates_supply=_supply, tou_rates_precal=_precal)
```

### O&R

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-or
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("or")
plot_representative_days("or", tou_rates=_bundled, tou_rates_supply=_supply, tou_rates_precal=_precal)
```

### PSEG-LI

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-psegli
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("psegli")
plot_representative_days("psegli", tou_rates=_bundled, tou_rates_supply=_supply, tou_rates_precal=_precal)
```

### RG&E

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-rge
_bundled, _supply, _precal = load_tou_rates("rge")
plot_representative_days("rge", tou_rates=_bundled, tou_rates_supply=_supply, tou_rates_precal=_precal)
```

## Report variables

```{python}
#| label: report-variables
import pickle
from pathlib import Path


def _tou_season_stats(tou: pl.DataFrame, season: str) -> dict:
    """Extract on/off-peak hours and rates for one season of a TOU profile."""
    s = tou.filter(pl.col("season") == season).sort("hour")
    rates = s["tou_rate"].unique().sort().to_list()
    off_rate, on_rate = rates[0], rates[-1]
    off_hours = s.filter(pl.col("tou_rate") == off_rate)["hour"].to_list()
    on_hours = s.filter(pl.col("tou_rate") == on_rate)["hour"].to_list()
    return {
        "off_rate": off_rate,
        "on_rate": on_rate,
        "off_hours": off_hours,
        "on_hours": on_hours,
        "on_duration": len(on_hours),
        "on_start": on_hours[0],
        "ratio": on_rate / off_rate,
    }


def _rep_day_mc_stats(utility: str) -> dict:
    """Compute average hourly MC stats for each representative day type."""
    rep = select_representative_days(mc_all.filter(pl.col("utility") == utility))
    stats: dict = {}
    for season in ["Summer", "Winter"]:
        for cap_level in ["Low-cost Day", "High-cost Day"]:
            subset = rep.filter(
                (pl.col("season") == season) & (pl.col("cap_level") == cap_level)
            )
            tag = f"{season.lower()}_{cap_level.split('-')[0].lower()}"
            energy = subset.filter(pl.col("mc_type") == "Energy")["value"]
            cap_types = ["Gen Capacity", "Bulk Tx", "Dist+Sub-Tx"]
            cap = subset.filter(pl.col("mc_type").is_in(cap_types))
            cap_hourly = cap.group_by("hour").agg(pl.col("value").sum())["value"]
            stats[f"{tag}_energy_avg"] = energy.mean()
            stats[f"{tag}_energy_max"] = energy.max()
            stats[f"{tag}_cap_avg"] = cap_hourly.mean()
            stats[f"{tag}_cap_min"] = cap_hourly.min()
            stats[f"{tag}_cap_max"] = cap_hourly.max()
    return stats


report_vars: dict = {}
_cenhud_bundled, _, _ = load_tou_rates("cenhud")
for season in ["Summer", "Winter"]:
    stats = _tou_season_stats(_cenhud_bundled, season)
    prefix = f"cenhud_{season.lower()}"
    for k, val in stats.items():
        report_vars[f"{prefix}_{k}"] = val

cenhud_mc = _rep_day_mc_stats("cenhud")
for k, val in cenhud_mc.items():
    report_vars[f"cenhud_{k}"] = val

report_vars["cenhud_winter_start_later"] = (
    report_vars["cenhud_winter_on_start"] - report_vars["cenhud_summer_on_start"]
)
report_vars["cenhud_offpeak_winter_minus_summer"] = (
    report_vars["cenhud_winter_off_rate"] - report_vars["cenhud_summer_off_rate"]
)
report_vars["cenhud_onpeak_winter_minus_summer"] = (
    report_vars["cenhud_winter_on_rate"] - report_vars["cenhud_summer_on_rate"]
)

Path("../cache").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
Path("../cache/tou_report_variables.pkl").write_bytes(pickle.dumps(report_vars))
```
